title: 10 Pounds
description: The front shows Al-Rifai Mosque in Cairo with Pharaoh Khafre. This is the 2025-issued variant of the long-running P-73 type with the 2022–2026 signature period.
body: The 10-pound note issued by the Central Bank of Egypt in 2025 is a continuation of the long-running P-73 series, which has been in production across multiple signature periods since the 1990s. The obverse displays the Al-Rifa'i Mosque, a landmark of Cairo's Islamic Cairo district. The reverse features the diorite statue of Pharaoh Khafre accompanied by the falcon god Horus. The note is printed in violet, brown, red, and yellow tones on cotton paper measuring 150 × 70 mm. Security features include a solid security thread, a windowed demetallized security thread with denomination, an Eye of Horus see-through registration device, and a watermark of King Khafre with electrotype numeral 10.

highlights.reason: Ongoing circulation issue continuing the long-running P-73 definitive series with updated date and signature.
highlights.design: Obverse: Al-Rifa'i Mosque, Cairo. Reverse: Diorite statue of Pharaoh Khafre with the Horus falcon.
highlights.security: Solid and windowed demetallized security thread, Eye of Horus registration device, Khafre watermark with electrotype 10.

title: 20 Zlotych
description: The front features Bolesław Chrobry based on a 19th-century etching. It is a commemorative issue for the millennium of his coronation, with details from the Gniezno bronze doors.
body: Issued on 5 November 2025 by the Narodowy Bank Polski (NBP), this commemorative collector banknote marks the millennium of the coronation of Bolesław I the Brave, Poland's first king, in 1025. The obverse depicts Bolesław Chrobry based on a graphic by Rudolf Fryderyk Friedlein (c. 1857) inspired by a portrait by Marcello Bacciarelli. The reverse draws on the bronze Gniezno Cathedral Doors, featuring scenes of Saint Adalbert's body and the Lance of Saint Maurice. Security features include a crown-shaped window cut, a gold-and-silver security stripe, microtext on both sides, and UV-reactive scenes. The note measures 170 × 85 mm — the largest in NBP's collector series — with a mintage capped at 70,000 pieces.

highlights.reason: Commemorates the 1,000th anniversary of Bolesław I the Brave's coronation in 1025, Poland's first royal crowning.
highlights.design: Obverse: Bolesław Chrobry after 19th-century etching. Reverse: Scenes from the Gniezno bronze doors, Princes Polonie coin, Lance of Saint Maurice.
highlights.security: Crown-shaped window cut, gold-and-silver security stripe with door details, microtext, UV-reactive imagery, watermark of Gniezno Door fragment.

title: 100 Cents
description: The front shows a stylized Chinese dragon with clouds and red lanterns. It is a non-circulating numismatic note for the Chinese Lunar Year of the Dragon.
body: The Reserve Bank of Fiji issued its 100-cent numismatic polymer banknote on 8 August 2023 to celebrate the Chinese Lunar Year of the Dragon (2024). Produced by De La Rue on Safeguard® polymer substrate, it measures 145 × 70 mm. The reverse shows a stylized Chinese dragon surrounded by white and yellow clouds, accompanied by a red lantern and the Chinese character for 'dragon,' with the inscription 'Good luck in the year of the dragon.' The obverse features a red hibiscus flower, the Fijian coat of arms, and a color-shifting hibiscus security device. A holographic foil strip incorporates wave patterns, the numeral 100, a Fiji Banded Iguana, and coral motifs. Limited to 950,000 pieces.

highlights.reason: Non-circulating numismatic issue celebrating the Chinese Lunar Year of the Dragon (2024), targeting collector and Asian numismatic markets.
highlights.design: Reverse: Stylized Chinese dragon with clouds, red lantern, and 'good luck' inscription. Obverse: Hibiscus flower, Fijian coat of arms.
highlights.security: De La Rue Safeguard® polymer substrate, holographic foil strip with wildlife motifs, Spark® color-shifting OVI hibiscus device.

title: 2 Dollars
description: The front shows Philip Goldson with the national coat of arms and Antelope Falls. A distinctive feature is the waterfall theme tied to Belizean natural landmarks.
body: The Central Bank of Belize revealed its new banknote family on 23 January 2025, with the series entering circulation on 4 August 2025. The redesigned $2 note is the first Belizean currency not to feature a British monarch as its primary portrait, replacing Queen Elizabeth II with Hon. Philip Stanley Wilberforce Goldson — the longest-serving member of the Belize House of Representatives, the first Leader of the Opposition, and the first Belizean statesman to address the United Nations. Goldson was posthumously awarded the Order of the National Hero. The obverse also displays the national coat of arms and an image of Antelope Falls at Mayflower Bocawina National Park; the reverse shows Sapodilla Falls at Elijio Panti National Park.

highlights.reason: Part of a full redesign of Belizean currency replacing the British monarch with national heroes; first Belize note to feature exclusively Belizean figures.
highlights.design: Obverse: Philip Goldson portrait, national coat of arms, Antelope Falls (Mayflower Bocawina National Park). Reverse: Sapodilla Falls (Elijio Panti National Park).
highlights.security: PUREIMAGE™ holographic security thread, Enhanced GEMINI™ UV patterns, enhanced watermarks, color-shifting security thread.

title: 5 Dollars
description: The front shows George Cadle Price with the national coat of arms and the National Assembly building in Belmopan. It is distinguished by the civic and historic architecture references on the note.
body: The redesigned Belize $5 note, unveiled on 23 January 2025 and entering circulation on 4 August 2025, honors Rt. Hon. George Cadle Price — Belize's first Prime Minister, founder of the People's United Party, and recipient of the Order of the National Hero. Price led Belize to independence in 1981. The red-toned note (140 × 70 mm) displays Price's portrait alongside the national coat of arms and the National Assembly building at Independence Plaza in Belmopan on the obverse. The reverse depicts the Belize City Peninsula and Haulover Creek, the Supreme Court building, and Saint John's Cathedral, the oldest Anglican church in Central America.

highlights.reason: Part of the 2025 Belizean currency redesign celebrating national heroes; honors George Cadle Price as the founding father of independent Belize.
highlights.design: Obverse: George Cadle Price portrait, national coat of arms, National Assembly building in Belmopan. Reverse: Belize City Peninsula, Haulover Creek, Supreme Court, Saint John's Cathedral.
highlights.security: PUREIMAGE™ holographic security thread, Enhanced GEMINI™ UV patterns, enhanced watermarks, color-shifting security thread.

title: 10 Dollars
description: The front shows Philip Goldson with the national coat of arms and a green heron. Its distinctive theme is Belizean birdlife, echoed by the toucan and hummingbirds referenced on the note.
body: The Central Bank of Belize unveiled its new family of banknotes on 23 January 2025, with the designs entering circulation on 4 August 2025. The 10-dollar note (Pick B333a, dated 1 February 2025) is printed in green on paper by De La Rue and measures 140 × 70 mm. The obverse features Order of National Hero Philip Goldson alongside the national coat of arms and a green heron, with the denomination as a see-through registration device. The reverse celebrates Belizean birdlife with a keel-billed toucan, a long-billed hermit hummingbird, and a scaly-breasted hummingbird. Security features include a holographic foil stripe bearing the denomination, a windowed security thread with demetallised elements, and a portrait watermark of Philip Goldson with electrotype '10'.

highlights.reason: Series modernisation to celebrate Belize's national heritage and incorporate enhanced anti-counterfeiting technology; honours Order of National Hero Philip Goldson
highlights.design: Obverse: Philip Goldson, coat of arms, green heron; Reverse: keel-billed toucan, long-billed hermit hummingbird, scaly-breasted hummingbird
highlights.security: Holographic foil stripe; windowed security thread with demetallised elements; portrait watermark with electrotype denomination

title: 20 Dollars
description: The front shows George Cadle Price with the national coat of arms and Baird's tapir. A distinctive element is the wildlife focus, especially the tapir and jaguar imagery.
body: Part of the Central Bank of Belize's 2025 series revealed on 23 January 2025 and introduced into circulation on 4 August 2025, the 20-dollar note (Pick B334a, dated 1 February 2025) is printed in brown on paper by De La Rue and measures 140 × 70 mm. The obverse portrays Order of National Hero and first Prime Minister George Cadle Price alongside the national coat of arms and a Baird's tapir, Belize's national animal. The reverse highlights jungle wildlife: a jaguar, a green iguana, and a blue morpho butterfly. Security features include a holographic foil stripe with denomination and butterfly motif, a windowed security thread with jaguar imagery, and a watermark of George Cadle Price with electrotype '20'.

highlights.reason: Series modernisation honouring first Prime Minister George Cadle Price and promoting Belizean national identity and wildlife
highlights.design: Obverse: George Cadle Price, coat of arms, Baird's tapir; Reverse: jaguar, green iguana, blue morpho butterfly
highlights.security: Holographic foil stripe with butterfly; windowed thread with jaguar head and demetallised '20'; portrait watermark with electrotype denomination

title: 50 Dollars
description: The front shows Philip Goldson with the national coat of arms and the Mask Temple at Lamanai. It is distinguished by its strong Maya archaeological theme.
body: The 50-dollar note (Pick B335a, dated 1 February 2025) is part of the Central Bank of Belize's redesigned series unveiled 23 January 2025 and circulated from 4 August 2025. Printed in burgundy on paper by De La Rue and measuring 150 × 75 mm, it focuses on Belize's Mayan archaeological heritage. The obverse features Order of National Hero Philip Goldson, the national coat of arms, and the iconic Mask Temple at the Lamanai archaeological site in northern Belize. The reverse showcases further Mayan monuments: the summit of Caana at Caracol, El Castillo at Xunantunich, and a segment of the Xunantunich east frieze. Security features include a holographic foil stripe with denomination and the Mask Temple, a windowed thread bearing '50' and the Mask Temple, and a portrait watermark of Philip Goldson with electrotype '50'.

highlights.reason: Series modernisation celebrating Belize's Mayan archaeological legacy and honouring national hero Philip Goldson
highlights.design: Obverse: Philip Goldson, coat of arms, Mask Temple at Lamanai; Reverse: Caana ruins at Caracol, El Castillo at Xunantunich, Xunantunich east frieze
highlights.security: Holographic foil stripe with Mask Temple; windowed thread with Mask Temple and '50'; portrait watermark with electrotype denomination

title: 100 Dollars
description: The front shows George Cadle Price with the national coat of arms and a queen triggerfish. A distinctive feature is the marine theme connected to the Great Blue Hole and reef life.
body: The 100-dollar note (Pick B336a, dated 1 February 2025) is the highest denomination in the Central Bank of Belize's 2025 series, unveiled 23 January 2025 and placed into circulation on 4 August 2025. Printed in blue on paper by De La Rue and measuring 150 × 75 mm, it celebrates Belize's marine biodiversity. The obverse shows Order of National Hero George Cadle Price, the national coat of arms, and a queen triggerfish (Balistes vetula), Belize's national fish. The reverse depicts the Belize Barrier Reef ecosystem: a hawksbill sea turtle, the famous Great Blue Hole, French angelfish, a nurse shark, and grooved brain coral. Security features include an IGNITE® windowed security thread, a holographic foil stripe with the hawksbill sea turtle, and a portrait watermark of George Cadle Price.

highlights.security: IGNITE® windowed security thread; holographic foil stripe with hawksbill sea turtle; portrait watermark with electrotype denomination

title: 1,000 Pesos
description: The front shows Hermila Galindo, Carmen Serdán, and Francisco I. Madero. This commemorative variant adds the "100 Aniversario 1925–2025" overprint for the 100th anniversary of Banco de México.
body: The Banco de México 1,000-peso Series G banknote (Pick B718, predominantly grey, 153 × 65 mm, cotton substrate) was originally introduced into circulation on 19 November 2020 to commemorate the centenary of the Mexican Revolution of 1910. It is the first Mexican banknote to feature three portrait subjects simultaneously: feminist and revolutionary journalist Hermila Galindo, political activist Carmen Serdán, and President Francisco I. Madero, accompanied by an engraving of a locomotive. The reverse depicts the UNESCO-recognised natural and cultural heritage site of Calakmul in Campeche. Security features include RAPID® micro-optic thread with kinetic 3D effects, Sparklive® optically variable numerals, intaglio printing, and Multicode® thread.

highlights.reason: Commemorates the centenary of the Mexican Revolution of 1910, honouring its democratic and equality ideals through the figures of Hermila Galindo, Carmen Serdán, and Francisco I. Madero; first Mexican banknote with three portrait subjects
highlights.design: Obverse: Hermila Galindo, Carmen Serdán, Francisco I. Madero, and a revolutionary-era locomotive; Reverse: Calakmul rainforest with jaguar, ceiba, and zapote trees
highlights.security: RAPID® micro-optic security thread with 3D movement; Sparklive® colour-shifting numerals; intaglio printing; Multicode® thread; watermark; fluorescent inks

title: 10 Caribbean Guilders
description: The front shows a gray angelfish and the Klein Curaçao lighthouse. Its distinctive feature is the marine-and-lighthouse identity of the new Caribbean guilder series.
body: The 10 Caribbean Guilder note entered circulation on 31 March 2025, replacing the Netherlands Antillean Guilder. Predominantly yellow and measuring 147 x 66 mm, the vertical front depicts a gray angelfish alongside a Queen Conch shell, celebrating the marine biodiversity of the Leeward islands. The horizontal reverse shows the iconic lighthouse on Klein Curacao, the uninhabited islet southeast of Curacao. Security features include a MOTION SURFACE stripe, a RAPID HD Detect micro-optic thread, a compass watermark, SPARK Flow color-shifting ink, and raised intaglio lines for visually impaired users. Printed on cotton paper by Crane Currency, the series was developed with input from CARMABI and the Sint Maarten Nature Foundation.

highlights.reason: Replacement of obsolete Netherlands Antillean Guilder following the 2010 monetary union between Curacao and Sint Maarten.
highlights.design: Vertical front with gray angelfish and Queen Conch shell on yellow background; horizontal reverse with Klein Curacao lighthouse.
highlights.security: MOTION SURFACE 3D stripe, RAPID HD Detect micro-optic thread, SPARK Flow color-shifting shell, compass+XCG watermark, see-through register, raised intaglio tactile lines.

title: 20 Caribbean Guilders
description: The front shows a spotted eagle ray. A distinctive feature is the Sint Maarten coastal setting of Simpson Bay in the new Caribbean guilder series.
body: The 20 Caribbean Guilder note, predominantly blue and issued on 31 March 2025, features two spotted eagle rays on the vertical front alongside a Yellow Cowry shell. The spotted eagle ray, common in Caribbean waters, represents the shared sea between Curacao and Sint Maarten. The horizontal reverse depicts Simpson Bay Lagoon in Sint Maarten, one of the largest inland bays in the Caribbean. All notes measure 147 x 66 mm and are printed on cotton paper by Crane Currency. The note replaces the Netherlands Antillean Guilder, legal tender since before the 2010 dissolution of the Netherlands Antilles.

title: 50 Caribbean Guilders
description: The front shows a green sea turtle. Its distinctive element is the Curaçao seascape at Grote Knip, reinforcing the marine identity of the series.
body: The 50 Caribbean Guilder note, issued on 31 March 2025, is the green denomination in the series. Its vertical front portrays a green sea turtle alongside a Favored Tellin shell. The horizontal reverse showcases Playa Kenepa Grandi (Grote Knip), one of Curacao's most celebrated beaches. The series was designed in collaboration with CARMABI (Curacao) and the Sint Maarten Nature Foundation, which each received a conservation donation from the CBCS.

title: 100 Caribbean Guilders
description: The front shows a stoplight parrotfish. A distinctive feature is the Court House in Sint Maarten, linking marine life with island civic heritage.
body: The 100 Caribbean Guilder note, the red denomination in the inaugural series issued on 31 March 2025, displays the stoplight parrotfish together with a Common Music Volute shell on the vertical front. The horizontal reverse features the Courthouse of Sint Maarten in Philipsburg. All notes share dimensions of 147 x 66 mm and are printed by Crane Currency. The series introduced a vertical front orientation, a first for this monetary union.

title: 200 Caribbean Guilders
description: The front shows a longsnout seahorse. It is distinguished by the Queen Emma Bridge motif, tying the note to Curaçao's best-known landmark.
body: The 200 Caribbean Guilder is the purple highest denomination in the inaugural series, issued on 31 March 2025. Its vertical front features two longsnout seahorses alongside a Giant Tun shell. The horizontal reverse showcases the Queen Emma Bridge in Willemstad, Curacao, the renowned pontoon swing bridge connecting the Punda and Otrobanda districts. The notes are pegged to the US dollar at a rate of 1 USD = 1.79 XCG.

title: 5 Pounds
description: The front shows Charles III with the Falkland Islands outline, coat of arms, and black-browed albatrosses. A distinctive feature is the polymer wildlife-and-island identity of the new series.
body: The Government of the Falkland Islands issued its £5 polymer note on Falklands Day, 14 August 2025 — the first major redesign in over 40 years and the first series to feature King Charles III. Produced by De La Rue on SAFEGUARD® polymer substrate, the red-coloured note shows Charles III on the obverse alongside black-browed albatrosses and a king penguin, with the Falkland Islands outline and coat of arms. The reverse highlights the islands' wildlife, depicting five penguin species, the national flower pale maiden, and Steeple Jason Island. Security features include a transparent king penguin window with iridescent ink, ultraviolet and infrared inks, and embossed tactile dots for visually impaired users.

title: 10 Pounds
description: The front shows Charles III with the Falkland Islands outline, coat of arms, and black-browed albatrosses. A distinctive feature is the polymer wildlife-and-island identity of the new series.
body: The £10 polymer note was introduced alongside the £5 and £20 on Falklands Day, 14 August 2025, forming the complete new Charles III series. Like all three denominations, it is printed by De La Rue on SAFEGUARD® polymer and shares the common obverse layout: a portrait of King Charles III, the Falkland Islands outline, coat of arms, and local seabirds. The green-coloured note replaces older paper issues bearing Queen Elizabeth II and will circulate in parallel with them until the legacy notes are withdrawn in 2026. Security features mirror the family standard: polymer substrate, transparent penguin window, iridescent ink, UV and infrared elements, and tactile embossing.

title: 20 Pounds
description: The front shows Charles III with the Falkland Islands outline, coat of arms, and black-browed albatrosses. A distinctive feature is the polymer wildlife-and-island identity of the new series.
body: The £20 polymer note completed the new Falkland Islands Charles III family launched on 14 August 2025 (Falklands Day). Printed by De La Rue on SAFEGUARD® polymer, the brown-coloured note carries the standard obverse design: King Charles III portrait, the Falkland Islands outline, coat of arms, and local wildlife. The reverse shows Steeple Jason Island and five penguin species native to the islands. The series marks the first note redesign in more than 40 years and transitions royal portraiture from Queen Elizabeth II to Charles III. The old paper notes remain legal tender but are scheduled for withdrawal in 2026. Security features include the polymer substrate, transparent penguin window, iridescent ink, UV/infrared printing, and tactile embossed dots.

title: 100 Quetzales
description: The front shows Francisco Marroquín. It commemorates the anniversaries of Banco de Guatemala and San Carlos de Borromeo University.
body: Banco de Guatemala presented this commemorative 100-quetzal note on 18 May 2026, with general circulation beginning 25 May 2026, printed by Polish state security printer PWPW S.A. on 100% cotton paper in a run of 15 million pieces. The note simultaneously marks three anniversaries: 100 years of central banking in Guatemala (monetary reform of 1926), 80 years since Banco de Guatemala's founding (1946), and 350 years since the establishment of Universidad de San Carlos de Borromeo (1676). The obverse features a portrait of Bishop Francisco Marroquín (1478–1563), Guatemala's first bishop, together with a flying quetzal bird. The reverse depicts the historic building of the Universidad de San Carlos. Security features include a windowed security thread with colour-shift from green to blue, Mayan microtext, watermark of Marroquín, Braille tactile elements, latent imagery, and optically variable ink.

highlights.security: Windowed colour-shift security thread (green to blue) with Great Jaguar pyramid, Mayan microtext, watermark, Braille tactile dots, latent imagery, optically variable ink

title: 100 Pesos
description: The front shows Juana Inés de la Cruz with the Colegio de San Ildefonso. A distinctive feature is the "100 Aniversario 1925–2025" overprint for the centenary of Banco de México.
body: Banco de México put the commemorative 100-peso Series G into circulation on 25 August 2025 to mark the central bank's centenary (1925–2025). The note is an overprinted variant of the existing polymer 100-peso bill with the addition of '100 Aniversario / 1925–2025' beneath the Banco de México monogram on the obverse. The front portrays Sor Juana Inés de la Cruz, the most celebrated poet and writer of New Hispanic literature, alongside the main courtyard of the Antiguo Colegio de San Ildefonso in Mexico City's Historic Centre. The red vertical note measures 132 × 65 mm. The reverse depicts pine, oak, and oyamel trees of the Monarch Butterfly Biosphere Reserve, a UNESCO World Heritage site.

title: 100 Rufiyaa
description: The front shows a group in traditional attire, including a woman working on a libaas neckline. A distinctive feature is the early Dhivehi script reference on the note.
body: The Maldives Monetary Authority issued this 100-rufiyaa polymer note (catalog B227b) bearing the date 31 July 2025 and the signature of Governor Ahmed Munawar. It is a signature-and-date update to the existing polymer family first introduced in January 2025. The obverse depicts a seated woman in traditional Libaas dress performing Hiru — the intricate neckline-threading craft — capturing Maldivian artisanal heritage. The reverse shows the Dambidu Lomafanu, an early Dhivehi palm-leaf scripture. The note is printed on De La Rue Safeguard polymer substrate and retains the full prior security architecture.

title: 100,000 Tögrög
description: The front shows Chinggis Khaan against a mountainous backdrop. It commemorates the 100th anniversaries of the People's Revolution and the tögrög currency.
body: Mongolbank introduced this 100,000-tögrög commemorative note on 9 December 2025, marking a dual centenary: the 100th anniversary of the People's Revolution of 1921 and the 100th anniversary of the tögrög, which entered circulation on 9 December 1925. The obverse features Chinggis Khaan against a mountainous backdrop alongside the Soyombo national symbol and a flaming green-to-gold SPARK security element. The reverse shows the Founding Seven of the People's Revolution including Damdin Sukhbaatar, plus a map of Mongolia. The note is printed in orange, brown, red, and blue.

highlights.reason: Dual centenary commemorative: 100th anniversary of the Mongolian People's Revolution and 100th anniversary of the introduction of the tögrög (9 December 1925).
highlights.design: Obverse: Chinggis Khaan, Soyombo symbol, mountain range, warrior-on-horseback registration device; reverse: portraits of the Founding Seven of the People's Revolution, map of Mongolia.
highlights.security: Flaming green-to-gold SPARK optically variable element; windowed security thread with demetalized text.

title: 10 Zlotych
description: The front shows Duke Mieszko I. A distinctive feature is the 2025-dated refresh of the standard note with the silver denar motif.
body: Narodowy Bank Polski issued this updated 10-złoty circulation note (catalog B859c) bearing the date 30 January 2025 and new signatures of Governor Glapiński and Treasurer Jaroszek. It is a standard periodic refresh of the existing Royal Piast dynasty series in circulation since 1995, with no redesign of the artwork. The obverse depicts Duke Mieszko I, Poland's first historically documented ruler, flanked by rosettes from Gniezno Cathedral. The reverse shows a silver denar from Mieszko I's reign, framed by stylised columns from the Benedictine Abbey in Tyniec. The note measures 120×60 mm in dark brown and green.

highlights.reason: Routine circulation series refresh: new date (30 January 2025) and updated governor/treasurer signatures, no change in design.
highlights.design: Obverse: portrait of Duke Mieszko I with Gniezno Cathedral rosettes; reverse: Mieszko I silver denar flanked by Benedictine Abbey columns.
highlights.security: Watermark (crown in oval); intaglio raised print; security thread; colour-shifting ink.

title: 20 Zlotych
description: The front shows King Bolesław I Chrobry. A distinctive feature is the 2025-dated refresh of the standard note with the medieval seal motif.
body: Narodowy Bank Polski released a 20-złoty collector commemorative note (catalog BNP829a) on 5 November 2025, celebrating the 1,000th anniversary of the coronation of Bolesław I Chrobry. A circulation date-and-signature variant (B860c, dated 19 March 2025) was also confirmed. The collector note features a portrait bust of Bolesław resting on a sword hilt with an eagle-and-shield motif on the obverse. The reverse reproduces scenes from the famous Gniezno Doors: the ransom of St. Adalbert's body, its procession, and burial at Gniezno. A crown-shaped die-cut window is punched through the note. Limited to 70,000 pieces, it was named Best Commemorative Banknote of 2025 by the International Bank Note Society.

highlights.reason: 1,000th anniversary of the coronation of Bolesław I Chrobry as Poland's first king (1025–2025); circulation date variant also exists (19 March 2025).
highlights.design: Obverse: portrait bust of Bolesław Chrobry with sword hilt and eagle-on-shield; reverse: Gniezno Door scenes; crown-shaped die-cut window.
highlights.security: Crown-shaped die-cut window through the paper; intaglio raised print; watermark; security thread.

title: 2,000 Rupees
description: The front shows the Central Bank of Sri Lanka building in Colombo. It commemorates the bank's 75th anniversary under the motto "Stability for Prosperity."
body: The Central Bank of Sri Lanka (CBSL) issued this Rs. 2,000 circulated commemorative note on 29 August 2025 to mark its 75th anniversary (1950–2025), themed 'Stability for Prosperity'. It is the fifth commemorative currency note in CBSL history and enters general circulation with a planned print run of 50 million pieces. The obverse features the Central Bank headquarters building in Colombo, the Colombo Lighthouse Clock Tower, and the Colombo skyline silhouette alongside the 75th anniversary logo. The reverse displays a stylised map of Sri Lanka, a water lily, the lion holding a sword from the national coat of arms, and the anniversary logo. The note measures 151×67 mm in a predominantly blue colour scheme.

highlights.reason: 75th anniversary of the Central Bank of Sri Lanka (established 1950), under the theme 'Stability for Prosperity'; fifth commemorative note in CBSL's history.
highlights.design: Obverse: CBSL headquarters, Colombo Lighthouse Clock Tower, Colombo skyline, 75th anniversary logo; reverse: stylised map of Sri Lanka, water lily, lion-and-sword motif, anniversary logo.
highlights.security: Wide colour-shifting security strip with image of Colombo Clock Tower; watermark of lion holding sword; 100% coated cotton paper substrate.

title: 100 Kina
description: Arms and Parliament building with a memorial issue for the first anniversary of Sir Julius Chan.
body: The Bank of Papua New Guinea issued this commemorative 100 Kina note on 27 January 2026, exactly one year after the passing of Grand Chief Sir Julius Chan — the nation's first Finance Minister, second Prime Minister, and founding father of the Kina and Toea currency system. The obverse retains the iconic Parliament House and the National Crest featuring a bird of paradise on a Kundu drum with ceremonial spear. The reverse introduces a portrait of Sir Julius Chan alongside a New Ireland shark-caller standing in a canoe, a traditional Lakatoi sailing vessel, a Kina shell necklace, and a Toea shell armband. Printed on Guardian polymer by Note Printing Australia, it measures 150 × 70 mm in green and gold tones.

highlights.reason: Memorial issue commemorating the first anniversary of the death of Grand Chief Sir Julius Chan, founder of Papua New Guinea's Kina and Toea monetary system.
highlights.design: Obverse: Parliament House, National Crest (bird of paradise on Kundu drum). Reverse: portrait of Sir Julius Chan, New Ireland shark-caller in canoe, Lakatoi vessel, Kina shell necklace, Toea shell armband.
highlights.security: Polymer (Guardian) substrate; shadow-image watermark of the Bank logo; no security thread. Intaglio printing. Produced by Note Printing Australia.

body: Bank Indonesia introduced the 2022-series 20,000 Rupiah note on 17 August 2022 to mark the country's 77th Independence Day. The obverse features a portrait of Dr. G.S.S.J. Ratulangi, a national hero and the first Governor of Sulawesi, set against a background of the Indonesian coat of arms, the black orchid (Coelogyne pandurata), and a map of Indonesia. The reverse depicts a Tari Gong dancer from East Kalimantan performing against a backdrop of the Derawan Islands and a sea turtle. The note is predominantly green-blue in color and measures 141 × 65 mm. New-date variants bearing a 2026 date have subsequently entered circulation with updated signatures (Perry Warjiyo / Sri Mulyani Indrawati).

title: 50 Dirhams
description: Seven founding fathers with flag after signing the union document.
body: The Central Bank of the UAE launched the 50 Dirhams polymer note on 7 December 2021 to celebrate the country's 50th National Day (Golden Jubilee). It is the UAE's first polymer banknote, printed on De La Rue's SAFEGUARD® substrate by Oumolat Security Printing. Measuring 151 × 64 mm in shades of violet and blue, the obverse portrays Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan alongside Wahat Al Karama (the Martyrs' Memorial) and the seven founding fathers signing the union document. The reverse depicts Sheikh Zayed signing the union charter with the Etihad Museum in the background. Advanced intaglio printing and fluorescent blue UAE nation brand marks appear centrally. Braille embossing aids visually impaired users. New-date and new-signature variants from 2026 are the latest circulation issues.

highlights.reason: Commemorates the 50th anniversary (Golden Jubilee) of the UAE's founding on 2 December 1971; first UAE polymer banknote.
highlights.design: Obverse: Sheikh Zayed portrait, Wahat Al Karama memorial, seven founding fathers signing the union document. Reverse: Sheikh Zayed signing union charter, Etihad Museum.
highlights.security: De La Rue SAFEGUARD® polymer; transparent windows with Sheikh Zayed portrait and Wahat Al Karama; fluorescent blue UAE nation brand marks; advanced intaglio printing; Braille embossing.

title: 5 Taka
body: Bangladesh Bank introduced this redesigned 5 Taka note on 19 May 2026 as part of the new 'Historical and Archaeological Architecture of Bangladesh' series. Sheikh Mujibur Rahman's portrait, which had appeared on previous issues, is absent; instead the obverse features the historic Star (Tara) Mosque in Dhaka on the left, with Bangladesh's national flower, the water lily (Shapla), dominating the background. The reverse bears the 'Graffiti-2024' artwork commemorating the July 2024 student uprising. Measuring 117 × 60 mm, the note is signed by Finance Secretary Dr. Md. Khairuzzaman Majumder. The watermark shows a Royal Bengal Tiger head with an electrotype numeral '5'.

highlights.reason: Part of the new 'Historical and Archaeological Architecture of Bangladesh' series, replacing the previous Sheikh Mujibur Rahman series amid post-uprising political transition.
highlights.design: Obverse: Star (Tara) Mosque in Dhaka, water lily (Shapla) national flower. Reverse: 'Graffiti-2024' mural artwork commemorating the July 2024 uprising.
highlights.security: Watermark: Royal Bengal Tiger face with electrotype numeral '5' and government emblem; 2mm security thread with 'Bangladesh' microtext; microprinted text 'Bangladesh' on lower front.

title: 10 Taka
body: Bangladesh Bank released the redesigned 10 Taka note on 3 February 2026 as the second denomination to circulate from the new 'Historical and Archaeological Architecture of Bangladesh' series. The predominantly pink note measures 123 × 60 mm. The obverse shows the Baitul Mukarram National Mosque in Dhaka — the country's largest mosque — flanked by the national Shapla flower. The reverse carries the 'Graffiti-2024' artwork, a nod to the student-led uprising of July 2024. The note bears the signature of Governor Dr. Ahsan Habib Mansur and marks a clean break from earlier issues featuring Sheikh Mujibur Rahman.

highlights.reason: Second note released in the new 'Historical and Archaeological Architecture' series; first circulation issue of the series to bear a new-year date of 2026.
highlights.design: Obverse: Baitul Mukarram National Mosque, Dhaka; Shapla (water lily) national flower. Reverse: 'Graffiti-2024' mural.
highlights.security: Watermark: Royal Bengal Tiger face with numeral '10' and Bangladesh Bank monogram; 2mm windowed security thread shifting red-to-green when tilted, reading '৳10 দেশ তকা'; 'BANGLADESH BANK' microtext on both sides.

title: 500 Taka
body: The Bangladesh Bank issued the redesigned 500 Taka note in late 2025, with a 2026-dated variant following in 2026, both part of the 'Historical and Archaeological Architecture of Bangladesh' series. Rendered in green and red on a 152 × 65 mm substrate, the obverse depicts the Shaheed Minar — the iconic Language Movement Martyrs' Monument in Dhaka — alongside the Shapla water lily, with the denomination printed in optically variable ink. The reverse shows the Supreme Court building in Dhaka with the bank logo. The notes bear the signature of Governor Ahsan Habib Mansur and replace the earlier series featuring Sheikh Mujibur Rahman.

highlights.reason: Part of the 'Historical and Archaeological Architecture of Bangladesh' series, acknowledging national monuments instead of political figures following the 2024 political transition.
highlights.design: Obverse: Shaheed Minar (Language Movement martyrs' monument), Shapla national flower, OVI denomination. Reverse: Supreme Court of Bangladesh building, bank logo.
highlights.security: Watermark: Royal Bengal Tiger, bank logo, numeral '500'; optically variable ink for denomination; windowed security thread; approximately 10 distinct security features.

title: 1.000 Taka
body: The largest Bangladeshi denomination, the 1,000 Taka, was relaunched on 1 June 2025 in the new 'Historical and Archaeological Architecture of Bangladesh' series, with a 2026-dated variant subsequently issued. The violet-purple note (160 × 70 mm) shows the National Martyrs' Monument at Savar — built to honor those who died in the 1971 Liberation War — alongside Shapla water lily motifs on the obverse. The reverse features the Jatiya Sangsad Bhaban, the iconic National Parliament building in Dhaka designed by Louis Kahn. The denomination is rendered in optically variable ink. This series removed the portrait of Sheikh Mujibur Rahman following Bangladesh's 2024 political changes.

highlights.reason: Flagship denomination of the new 'Historical and Archaeological Architecture' series, eliminating the portrait of Sheikh Mujibur Rahman and highlighting national monuments.
highlights.design: Obverse: National Martyrs' Monument, Savar; Shapla (water lily) national flower; OVI denomination. Reverse: Jatiya Sangsad Bhaban (National Parliament), Dhaka.
highlights.security: Watermark: Royal Bengal Tiger, bank logo, denomination '1000'; windowed security thread; OVI numeral; tactile markers for visually impaired users.

title: 20 Yuan
body: The People's Bank of China released this 20 Yuan commemorative polymer note in December 2025, entering wide circulation for the Lunar New Year in January 2026. It is the third installment in the PBoC's planned 12-year Chinese Zodiac series (2024–2035), following the 2024 Dragon and 2025 Snake notes. The vertically oriented note (145 × 70 mm) is rendered in red and gold. The obverse features a stylized horse in the traditional Chinese paper-cut artistic style, the national emblem, decorative lanterns and daylily patterns, and a SPARK® optical variable denomination. The reverse depicts three children celebrating the Spring Festival with gongs and drums in front of traditional Mongolian yurt tents, with text in five ethnic scripts.

highlights.reason: Third note in the PBoC's 12-year Chinese Zodiac commemorative polymer series, celebrating the Year of the Horse (2026) and the Spring Festival.
highlights.design: Obverse: Stylized horse in paper-cut style, national emblem, lanterns, daylily patterns. Reverse: Children playing gongs and drums at Spring Festival, yurt tents in background, text in Mongolian, Tibetan, Uyghur, Zhuang, and Chinese.
highlights.security: Polymer substrate (no security thread or watermark); SPARK® optical variable device denomination; holographic ribbon; dynamic see-through transparent window; color-shifting elements.

title: 5.000 Pesos
description: Celia Sánchez Manduley (Heroína de la Sierra Maestra).
body: The Banco Central de Cuba placed the 5,000 Peso note into circulation on 1 April 2026 — initially in Havana, then gradually nationwide. Issued alongside a new 2,000 Peso note, these are the highest-denomination banknotes ever introduced in Cuba, driven by severe inflation that had rendered smaller notes impractical. The 150 × 70 mm note is predominantly blue and features, for the first time in Cuban numismatic history, a female patriot: Celia Sánchez Manduley, a key revolutionary figure and close collaborator of Fidel Castro. The reverse depicts the Memorial Celia Sánchez in Parque Lenin, Havana. Cuba's national flower, La Mariposa (white butterfly jasmine), appears on the note with a rainbow color-shift effect when the viewing angle changes.

highlights.reason: Issued in response to severe inflation that demanded high-value physical currency to facilitate everyday transactions; marks the first Cuban banknotes to feature female historical figures.
highlights.design: Obverse: Portrait of Celia Sánchez Manduley, Cuban revolutionary heroine; national flower La Mariposa with rainbow color-shift effect. Reverse: Memorial Celia Sánchez, Parque Lenin, Havana.
highlights.security: Special security paper meeting international standards; watermark of Celia Sánchez portrait with electrotype denomination; vertical security thread on right side with movement effect showing 'BCC' and denomination; tactile element in upper-right corner for visually impaired.

title: 100 Korun Českých
description: Printer. 100th Anniversary of the Czech Koruna (1919-2019).
body: The Czech National Bank issued this third commemorative 100 Korun note on 21 January 2026 to mark 100 years since the establishment of the first Czechoslovak central bank, the Národní banka Československá. The note (84 × 194 mm) is printed in dark blue intaglio and features a portrait of Vilém Pospíšil (1873–1942), the founding governor of that institution, on the obverse alongside the CNB headquarters topped by the Genius with a Lion statue, the middle coat of arms of the Czechoslovak Republic, and a cornucopia. The reverse depicts the corner of the former Městská spořitelna Pražská savings bank building in Prague, flanked by allegorical figures.

highlights.reason: Commemorates the centennial of the establishment of the Národní banka Československá (1926–2026), the first Czechoslovak central bank.
highlights.design: Obverse: Portrait of Vilém Pospíšil (founding governor), CNB headquarters with Genius statue, Czechoslovak coat of arms, cornucopia. Reverse: Corner of former Městská spořitelna Pražská building, Prague; allegorical genius figures.
highlights.security: 1.4mm windowed security thread (perpendicular to long side) with microtext '1873–1942'; stepped watermark portrait of Pospíšil; white-line watermark with '100' and treble clef; latent image showing '100' on tilt; intaglio printing.

title: 5 Dollars
body: The Reserve Bank of Fiji launched all five denominations of a fully redesigned polymer series on 2 January 2026, completing the country's transition to an all-polymer currency. The 5 Dollar note is produced on GUARDIAN™ polymer by Crane Currency. Its obverse features a Red-throated Lory parrot (Charmosyna amabilis), a vibrantly colored endemic bird. The reverse depicts a Vonu Dina (green sea turtle), an endangered native species. A transparent window contains a decorative salt basket design. The note forms part of a series in which front designs celebrate Fiji's iconic flora and fauna while reverse designs honor the nation's people, heritage, and resources.

highlights.reason: First full all-polymer banknote series for Fiji, replacing a mixed substrate series; introduces Crane Currency's MOTION SURFACE® micro-optic security and reflects Fiji's national identity.
highlights.design: Obverse: Red-throated Lory parrot (Charmosyna amabilis), endemic flora. Reverse: Vonu Dina (green sea turtle).
highlights.security: GUARDIAN™ polymer; MOTION SURFACE® micro-optic stripe featuring Fiji coat of arms and native plant leaves; CAMEO™ secondary transparent window with salt basket; G SWITCH™ color-shifting effect; IRISWITCH™ pearlescent band; tactile lines on short edges; embossed dots for visually impaired.

title: 10 Dollars
body: The Reserve Bank of Fiji introduced the redesigned 10 Dollar polymer note on 2 January 2026 as part of its landmark first all-polymer series. The obverse of this GUARDIAN™ substrate note features a fish illustration evoking Fiji's rich marine environment. The reverse is dedicated to environmental stewardship, depicting children planting mangroves along a coastline — a symbol of Fiji's commitment to climate resilience and coastal ecosystem protection. A transparent window contains a duck-shaped traditional Fijian vase motif. The note includes the MOTION SURFACE® micro-optic stripe — a world-first technology in a full polymer series — and tactile accessibility features that increase in number with denomination.

highlights.reason: Part of the 2026 all-polymer series replacing the previous mixed-substrate design; highlights environmental conservation and climate action as key national values.
highlights.design: Obverse: Native fish illustration, Fijian flora and fauna motifs. Reverse: Children planting mangroves on coastline.
highlights.security: GUARDIAN™ polymer; MOTION SURFACE® micro-optic stripe; CAMEO™ transparent window with duck-vase motif; G SWITCH™ color-shifting; IRISWITCH™ pearlescent band; tactile lines on edges; accessibility dots.

title: 20 Dollars
description: Foa artefact, Kacau ni Gau (Fiji petrel).
body: Fiji's redesigned 20 Dollar polymer note entered circulation on 2 January 2026, part of the Reserve Bank of Fiji's first fully polymer series. The obverse of the GUARDIAN™ substrate note depicts a bird in flight over waves, evoking the archipelago's seascape. The reverse displays the Reserve Bank of Fiji's head office building in Suva — a recognition of the central bank's role in monetary governance. A transparent window features a coconut scraper (tavā), a traditional Fijian domestic tool. Along with all denominations in the series, the note carries Crane Currency's MOTION SURFACE® micro-optic security stripe, and denomination-specific color transitions in the G SWITCH™ window.

highlights.reason: New all-polymer series for Fiji, with reverses celebrating national institutions and cultural heritage; this denomination acknowledges the Reserve Bank itself.
highlights.design: Obverse: Native bird in flight over ocean waves. Reverse: Reserve Bank of Fiji building, Suva.
highlights.security: GUARDIAN™ polymer; MOTION SURFACE® micro-optic stripe with coat of arms and native plant leaves; CAMEO™ transparent window with coconut scraper motif; G SWITCH™ color-shifting; IRISWITCH™ band; tactile lines and dots.

title: 50 Dollars
body: The 50 Dollar note introduced on 2 January 2026 is notable for its tribute to Fiji's Indo-Fijian community and the girmit legacy. The obverse features a liana flower, representing Fiji's tropical vegetation. The reverse honors a Girmitiya woman — a descendant of indentured laborers brought from India to Fiji between 1879 and 1916 — with the historical vessel Leonidas, the first ship to transport Indian indentured workers, depicted in the background. A transparent window carries a whale's tooth (tabua) necklace, a sacred object in traditional Fijian culture. The note is printed on GUARDIAN™ polymer by Crane Currency with a full suite of MOTION SURFACE® and G SWITCH™ security elements.

highlights.reason: New all-polymer series; this denomination specifically honors the girmit (indenture) history and the contributions of Indo-Fijian communities to the nation's development.
highlights.design: Obverse: Liana flower. Reverse: Traditional Girmitiya (Indo-Fijian) woman with the ship Leonidas in the background.
highlights.security: GUARDIAN™ polymer; MOTION SURFACE® micro-optic stripe; CAMEO™ transparent window with whale's tooth (tabua) necklace; G SWITCH™ denomination-specific color-shift; IRISWITCH™ band; tactile lines; accessibility dots.

title: 100 Dollars
body: The highest denomination in Fiji's new 2026 all-polymer series, the 100 Dollar note entered circulation on 2 January 2026. It celebrates indigenous Fijian (i-Taukei) culture through its central reverse image: a man in traditional ceremonial attire presenting a bilo (coconut-shell bowl) of yaqona (kava) — the Pacific's most significant ceremonial drink. The obverse displays a nanai (cicada) insect alongside the Fijian coat of arms and kava plant leaves. A transparent window features a golden cowrie shell necklace, a traditional symbol of chiefly status. Printed on GUARDIAN™ polymer by Crane Currency, the note features the most tactile accessibility markers of any denomination in the series.

highlights.reason: Highest denomination in the new all-polymer series; honors indigenous Fijian (i-Taukei) culture and the centrality of the yaqona (kava) ceremony to national identity.
highlights.design: Obverse: Nanai (cicada) insect, Fijian coat of arms, kava plant leaves. Reverse: i-Taukei man in traditional dress presenting kava in a bilo (coconut shell bowl).
highlights.security: GUARDIAN™ polymer; MOTION SURFACE® micro-optic stripe; CAMEO™ transparent window with golden cowrie shell necklace; G SWITCH™ color-shifting; IRISWITCH™ band; highest number of tactile lines and accessibility dots among all five denominations.

title: 50 Rupees
description: Mahatma Gandhi, Lion capital of Asoka column.
body: The Reserve Bank of India introduced this 50 Rupee note on 18 August 2017 as part of the Mahatma Gandhi (New) Series, replacing the earlier design. New-date variants with updated governor signatures have continued to be issued, including a 2026-dated version. Fluorescent blue in color and measuring 135 × 66 mm, the obverse bears the portrait of Mahatma Gandhi, the Ashoka Pillar emblem, and micro-text security elements. The reverse depicts the Hampi with Chariot motif — the iconic stone chariot (Garuda Ratha) at the Vittala Temple complex, a UNESCO World Heritage Site in Karnataka — along with the Swachh Bharat Abhiyan logo and slogan.

highlights.reason: Part of the RBI's Mahatma Gandhi New Series introduced following the 2016 demonetisation; new-date variants update signatories while retaining the design.
highlights.design: Obverse: Mahatma Gandhi portrait, Ashoka Pillar emblem. Reverse: Hampi with Chariot (Garuda Ratha stone chariot, Vittala Temple, Karnataka); Swachh Bharat logo.
highlights.security: Windowed demetalized security thread with 'भारत' (Bharat) and 'RBI'; latent image of '50' on right vertical band; Mahatma Gandhi watermark and electrotype '50'; see-through register; intaglio printing; optically variable ink denomination; fluorescent fibers; Braille-like square tactile mark.

title: 200 Rupees
description: Mahatma Gandhi, Lion capital of Asoka column.
body: The Reserve Bank of India launched the 200 Rupee note on 25 August 2017 as a new denomination filling a gap in the Mahatma Gandhi (New) Series between ₹100 and ₹500. The bright yellow note measures 146 × 66 mm. The obverse features the portrait of Mahatma Gandhi alongside the Ashoka Pillar emblem and raised intaglio printing. The reverse depicts the Great Stupa of Sanchi in Madhya Pradesh — an iconic Buddhist monument dating to the 3rd century BCE and a UNESCO World Heritage Site — reflecting India's ancient cultural heritage. New-date variants bearing updated governor signatures, including a 2026 version, continue to circulate.

highlights.reason: New denomination introduced to fill the gap between ₹100 and ₹500 in the Mahatma Gandhi New Series following demonetisation in 2016.
highlights.design: Obverse: Mahatma Gandhi portrait, Ashoka Pillar emblem, raised intaglio text. Reverse: Great Stupa of Sanchi, Madhya Pradesh (3rd century BCE Buddhist monument).
highlights.security: Green-to-blue color-shifting windowed security thread with 'भारत' and 'RBI'; latent image of '200' on vertical band; see-through register with floral denomination design; Mahatma Gandhi and electrotype '200' watermarks; raised intaglio printing; angular bleed lines for tactile identification; optically variable ink '200' (green to blue).

title: 20.000 Rupiah
description: Dr. G. S. S. J. Ratulangi, arms, outline of Indonesia.
body: Bank Indonesia's 2022-series 20,000 Rupiah note was issued on 17 August 2022 to celebrate Indonesia's 77th Independence Day anniversary. A new-date variant bearing a 2026 date (B621e) has been confirmed, carrying updated signatures. The obverse features Dr. G.S.S.J. Ratulangi, a national independence hero and the first governor of Sulawesi Province, set against the Indonesian coat of arms, a black orchid, and a national map. The reverse depicts a Tari Gong dancer from East Kalimantan and a sea turtle off the Derawan Islands. The predominantly green-blue note (141 × 65 mm) forms part of the complete 2022 series which replaced the 2016 rupiah family.

highlights.reason: 2026-dated variant of the 2022 independence anniversary series, updating the circulating note with new signatures while retaining the existing design.
highlights.design: Obverse: Dr. G.S.S.J. Ratulangi portrait, Indonesian coat of arms, black orchid, map. Reverse: Tari Gong dancer from East Kalimantan, Derawan Islands sea turtle.
highlights.security: Windowed demetalized security thread 'BI 20000'; Oto Iskandar di Nata watermark; UV fluorescence; phosphorescent ink; infrared ink; magnetic security thread; intaglio printing.

title: 200 Naira
body: The Central Bank of Nigeria unveiled redesigned 200, 500, and 1,000 Naira notes in November 2022, with the updated 200 Naira entering circulation by 15 December 2022. Subsequent signature-change variants have continued through 2026. The 200 Naira note measures 151 × 78 mm and is distinctly rendered in red, pink, and orange — a significant shift from its previous muted green-brown palette. The obverse retains the portrait of Sir Ahmadu Bello, the Sardauna of Sokoto and a founding leader of Nigeria's First Republic. The reverse depicts agricultural commodities, livestock farming, and the Nigerian coat of arms, celebrating the country's agrarian economy.

highlights.reason: Redesigned in 2022 at President Buhari's approval to reduce counterfeiting and curb corruption by making existing notes harder to hoard; new-signature variants updated through 2026.
highlights.design: Obverse: Sir Ahmadu Bello (Sardauna of Sokoto) portrait. Reverse: Agricultural commodities, livestock, Nigerian coat of arms, yellow trumpet flowers.
highlights.security: Hand-engraved portrait; windowed metallic security thread with 'CBN' inscription; CBN 200 watermark; portrait watermark; optically variable ink (blue to green); embossed coat of arms gold patch; see-through register; raised intaglio printing; UV-fluorescent security ink.

title: 100 Kina
description: Arms, Parliament building (Port Moresby). First Anniversary of the Passing of Sir Julius Chan (2025-2026).
body: This is the regular circulation 100 Kina note issued by the Bank of Papua New Guinea bearing a 2026 date, distinct from the Sir Julius Chan commemorative issue. The standard 100 Kina has been the flagship denomination of Papua New Guinea's polymer note series for many years. The obverse features Parliament House in Port Moresby and the National Crest of Papua New Guinea — a bird of paradise perched on a Kundu drum with a ceremonial spear. The reverse carries traditional cultural imagery representing the diversity of Papua New Guinea's people, including canoes and traditional ceremonial elements. The note is printed on Guardian polymer substrate by Note Printing Australia.

highlights.reason: Standard annual circulation issue of the flagship 100 Kina denomination, updated with a 2026 date and current governor signatures.
highlights.design: Obverse: Parliament House, Port Moresby; National Crest (bird of paradise on Kundu drum). Reverse: Traditional Papua New Guinean cultural imagery including canoes and ceremonial elements.
highlights.security: Guardian polymer substrate; shadow-image watermark; intaglio printing; printed by Note Printing Australia.

title: 5 Pounds
body: The Government of Saint Helena issued this redesigned 5 Pound polymer note, which entered official circulation on 22 January 2026. It is the smallest denomination of a new three-note series (£5, £10, £20) that replaced the previous King Charles III paper series with more secure and durable polymer notes. Printed on polymer by De La Rue and measuring 135 × 70 mm, the blue note bears the official portrait of King Charles III on the obverse. The reverse depicts two of Saint Helena's most beloved natural symbols: the endemic Saint Helena Ebony flower (Trochetiopsis ebenus) and Jonathan the Tortoise, the world's oldest known living land animal at over 190 years of age.

highlights.reason: Transition from paper to polymer currency featuring King Charles III; modernises the island's banknotes while preserving Saint Helena's distinct identity through natural heritage imagery.
highlights.design: Obverse: Official portrait of King Charles III. Reverse: Saint Helena Ebony flower (Trochetiopsis ebenus) and Jonathan the Tortoise (Aldabrachelys gigantea hololissa).
highlights.security: De La Rue polymer substrate; security features intrinsic to polymer construction (transparency, durability, counterfeit resistance).

title: 10 Pounds
body: The 10 Pound polymer note from the Government of Saint Helena entered circulation on 22 January 2026 as part of the new series alongside the £5 and £20 issues. Measuring 135 × 70 mm, it is produced on polymer by De La Rue and is rendered in red. Like all notes in this series, the obverse carries the official effigy of King Charles III. The reverse mirrors the theme of the companion denominations, featuring the Saint Helena Ebony (Trochetiopsis ebenus) — a critically endangered endemic flowering tree — and Jonathan the Tortoise, the Seychelles giant tortoise resident at Plantation House who serves as a global symbol of Saint Helena.

highlights.reason: New polymer series featuring King Charles III, replacing earlier paper notes; polymer substrate significantly increases durability and security for the island's small-circulation currency.
highlights.design: Obverse: Official portrait of King Charles III. Reverse: Saint Helena Ebony flower (Trochetiopsis ebenus) and Jonathan the Tortoise.
highlights.security: De La Rue polymer substrate; security features inherent to polymer (transparent elements, anti-counterfeiting printing).

title: 20 Pounds
body: The highest denomination in Saint Helena's new 2026 polymer series, the 20 Pound note entered official circulation on 22 January 2026. De La Rue printed the 135 × 70 mm note on polymer in a brown color scheme. The obverse shows the official royal portrait of King Charles III. The reverse consistently features the same natural imagery as the £5 and £10 notes — the Saint Helena Ebony flower and Jonathan the Tortoise — creating a unified visual identity across the series. The notes are signed by Governor Richards, Financial Secretary Yon, and Treasurer O'Bey/Francis.

highlights.reason: Largest denomination of the new polymer series; designed to give Saint Helena's currency a modern, durable format bearing the new monarch's portrait.
highlights.design: Obverse: Official portrait of King Charles III. Reverse: Saint Helena Ebony flower (Trochetiopsis ebenus) and Jonathan the Tortoise.
highlights.security: De La Rue polymer substrate; polymer-inherent anti-counterfeiting properties.

title: 50 Dirhams
description: Seven founding fathers with flag after signing the union document.
body: This is the 2026 new-date variant of the UAE's polymer 50 Dirhams note, originally introduced on 7 December 2021 as the UAE's Golden Jubilee commemorative issue and its first polymer banknote. The design remains unchanged from the 2021 issue: the 151 × 64 mm note is produced on De La Rue SAFEGUARD® polymer in violet and blue tones. The obverse depicts Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan, Wahat Al Karama, and the seven founding fathers. The reverse shows Sheikh Zayed signing the 1971 union charter at the Etihad Museum. Braille embossing, transparent windows with optically variable imagery, and advanced intaglio printing provide multi-layer authentication.

highlights.reason: Updated circulation variant of the 2021 Golden Jubilee polymer 50 Dirhams, reflecting standard practice of issuing new-date or new-signature versions while retaining the commemorative design.
highlights.design: Obverse: Sheikh Zayed portrait, Wahat Al Karama (Martyrs' Memorial), seven founding fathers. Reverse: Sheikh Zayed signing union charter, Etihad Museum.
highlights.security: De La Rue SAFEGUARD® polymer; transparent windows; fluorescent blue UAE nation brand marks; advanced intaglio printing; Braille embossing; denomination-specific color-shifting optically variable features.

title: 10 ZiG (Zimbabwe Gold)
description: Cape buffalo, Chiremba balancing rock formation.
body: The Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe introduced the upgraded ZiG 10 note on 7 April 2026 as part of the new 'Big 5' ZiG series, replacing the original 2024 ZiG notes that had suffered rapid wear. The Zimbabwe Gold (ZiG) currency, launched in April 2024 and linked to gold reserves, required a more durable redesign. The ZiG 10 features an African Buffalo (Cape buffalo) rendered in intaglio on the obverse, alongside the RBZ's three balancing rocks logo. The reverse depicts the Matopo Hills — the ancient granite domes and balancing rocks of Matobo National Park in southern Zimbabwe, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Security is enhanced over the 2024 series.

highlights.reason: Redesigned 'Big 5' upgrade of the original 2024 ZiG notes; banks and retailers had reported rapid fading and tearing; the RBZ issued a more durable note with enhanced security for the ZiG currency backed by gold and foreign reserves.
highlights.design: Obverse: African Buffalo (Cape buffalo), RBZ three balancing rocks logo. Reverse: Matopo Hills (Matobo National Park), Zimbabwe.
highlights.security: Intaglio-printed wildlife with tactile relief; magnetic security thread with denomination inscription; PEAK™ anti-copy element; SPARK® optical variable device showing Zimbabwe Bird; ColorShift™ element; see-through register; watermark of Zimbabwe Bird with denomination; tactile horizontal lines for visually impaired.

title: 20 ZiG (Zimbabwe Gold)
body: The ZiG 20 entered circulation on 7 April 2026 as the middle denomination of Zimbabwe's new 'Big 5' banknote series. The upgrade was prompted by widespread durability failures in the original 2024 ZiG notes. The obverse of the ZiG 20 features an African Elephant — the largest of the Big Five — rendered in high-relief intaglio printing, alongside the Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe's logo of three balancing rocks. The reverse depicts the Parliament of Zimbabwe building in Harare, linking Zimbabwe's wildlife heritage with its constitutional institutions. The note carries a full suite of modern security features including SPARK® optical variable device and PEAK™ anti-copy elements.

highlights.reason: Upgraded Big 5 redesign of the 2024 ZiG 20 note; addresses documented durability problems and introduces enhanced security features for the gold-backed ZiG currency.
highlights.design: Obverse: African Elephant, RBZ three balancing rocks logo. Reverse: Parliament of Zimbabwe, Harare.
highlights.security: Intaglio-printed elephant with tactile surface; magnetic security thread; PEAK™ anti-copy element; SPARK® optical variable Zimbabwe Bird; ColorShift™ device; see-through register; Zimbabwe Bird watermark with denomination; tactile lines for visually impaired.

title: 50 ZiG (Zimbabwe Gold)
body: The highest of the three initial 'Big 5' ZiG denominations launched on 7 April 2026, the ZiG 50 completes the first phase of Zimbabwe's upgraded gold-backed banknote series. The obverse depicts a Black Rhinoceros — one of Africa's most endangered mammals and a symbol of Zimbabwe's conservation efforts — alongside the RBZ logo. The reverse features a national landmark as published in the Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe's official gazette. The ZiG 50 carries the most comprehensive security package of the three initial denominations. Higher denominations (ZiG 100 featuring a Leopard and ZiG 200 featuring a Lion) are planned for later issuance.

highlights.reason: Highest-value note in the first tranche of the Big 5 ZiG series; part of the broader upgrade program addressing durability failures of the original 2024 ZiG notes and reinforcing confidence in Zimbabwe's gold-backed currency.
highlights.design: Obverse: Black Rhinoceros, RBZ three balancing rocks logo. Reverse: National landmark as gazetted by the Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe.
highlights.security: Intaglio-printed rhinoceros with tactile relief; magnetic security thread with denomination inscription; PEAK™ anti-copy element; SPARK® optical variable Zimbabwe Bird device; ColorShift™ element; see-through register; Zimbabwe Bird watermark; tactile lines and denomination-specific accessibility markers.
